science.title: Scientists create supercharged vitamin K that helps the brain heal itself
science.summary: Scientists in Japan have created powerful new vitamin K-based compounds that may help the brain regenerate lost neurons — a breakthrough that could one day change how diseases like Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s are treated. By combining vitamin K with components related to vitamin A, the researchers developed compounds t

science.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
science.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds.

science.title: 100-million-year-old bug had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
science.summary: Deep inside 100-million-year-old amber from Myanmar, scientists uncovered a bizarre ancient bug with clawed front legs that look more like a crab’s pincers than anything seen in modern insects. The discovery is so unusual that researchers say these crab-like “chelae” evolved independently in this lineage, making it onl

science.title: It’s Alive? Surprising Discovery Changes What We Know About Fog
science.summary: Scientists discovered that fog droplets can host living bacteria that grow and help remove harmful pollutants from the atmosphere, revealing fog as a surprisingly active microbial environment. Every breath you take may contain microscopic hitchhikers floating through the atmosphere. Scientists have known for years that

science.title: Brain Study Overturns Long-Held Beliefs About How Humans Learn Speech
science.summary: Scientists have discovered that the brain’s sensory systems play a much larger role in speech learning than previously believed. New research suggests that learning to speak a new language, or recovering speech after injury, relies more heavily on the brain’s sensory systems than on regions responsible for controlling

tech.title: Amazon Thinks the Future of Data Centers Depends on a Technical Problem It Just Solved
tech.summary: The tech giant says a breakthrough in data center networking has dramatically accelerated the flow of information through its massive cloud infrastructure.

tech.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
tech.summary: The botnet was reportedly tied to a Russia-based residential proxy network.

tech.title: Startup offers free home cleaning—if it can record it all for robot training
tech.summary: The latest twist in paying humans to wear head cameras for robot training data.

tech.title: After Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M
tech.summary: Chipmaker Groq is looking to raise $650 million in internal funding as it pivots from hardware to focus more on AI inference, the process of refining the way AI models respond to prompted requests, per Axios.

tech.title: Microsoft under fire for threatening security researcher with criminal investigation
tech.summary: A public spat between Microsoft and an independent security researcher reopens a long-running debate over who is responsible for securing software.

medicine.title: New drug could finally stop deadly fatty liver disease
medicine.summary: Scientists at UC San Diego have unveiled a potentially game-changing treatment for MASH, a severe fatty liver disease affecting millions worldwide. medicine.title: Teclistamab extends remission in relapsed myeloma, with 70% progression-free at 18 months

medicine.title: RNA therapy for genetic heart failure moves closer to patients after lab gains
medicine.summary: Using patient-derived cardiac tissue and stem cell-based models, the team of translational researchers demonstrated that targeting the genetic cause of disease improved cellular abnormalities and identified the biological pathways involved. The results of this study were published in Signal Transduction and Targeted Th

medicine.title: One in five patients achieve functional hepatitis B cure after 24 weeks of bepirovirsen
medicine.summary: In an editorial published in the New England Journal of Medicine, University of Michigan Health hepatologist Anna S. Lok, M.D., hails newly announced results of the B-Well clinical trials as "a major step toward a functional cure for hepatitis B virus infection."

medicine.title: Analysis reveals consumer wearables emerge as the new health care gatekeepers
medicine.summary: A News and Perspectives expert analysis on consumer wearable platforms' forays into the clinical health care space. mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. mathematics.title: How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics

space.title: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts return to Earth after being briefly 'stranded', wrapping up record-breaking mission (video)

space.title: Atlas V rocket launches 29 Amazon Leo internet satellites to orbit
space.summary: A United Launch Alliance Atlas V rocket launched 29 Amazon Leo internet satellites to orbit on Friday night (May 28).

astronomy.title: JWST Studies a Dark and Airless Super-Earth
astronomy.summary: There's a planet out there called LHS 3844 b, orbiting a star about 48 light-years away. The Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) found it in 2018 when the planet transited across the face of its star. astronomy.title: Red dwarf stars are cosmic killers that eat their own planets
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have discovered the first evidence that tiny red dwarf stars can devour their own planets.

astronomy.title: Close Encounter: Jupiter and Venus
astronomy.summary: The two brightest planets in our sky will be less than 2 degrees apart on June 9th at sunset. The post Close Encounter: Jupiter and Venus appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Lost in the Star Clouds — A Milky Way Odyssey
astronomy.summary: I share my "discovery" of a new Milky Way star cloud that's been staring at me for ages. The post Lost in the Star Clouds — A Milky Way Odyssey appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31
astronomy.summary: Venus and Jupiter — the Evening Star and the False Evening Star — draw closer together in the western twilight and point down to Mercury. The waxing Moon shows off its rich telescopic detail. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.title: AD Classics: Palmas 555 / Sordo Madaleno Arquitectos
architecture.summary: Palmas 555 is a building that stands out in the urban landscape of Mexico City due to its special volumetry and innovative design. This corporate office building was designed and constructed by Juan Sordo Madaleno together with José Adolfo Wiechers and José Ignacio de Abiega as Associate Architects in 1975.

architecture.title: Dezeen Debate features "proper dystopian" Prairie Ark Gallery
architecture.summary: The latest edition of our Dezeen Debate newsletter features the Prairie Ark gallery by Chinese architecture studio Büro Ziyu Zhuang, which resembles a flying saucer. construction.title: Illinois starts critical $164M I-80 bridge project

construction.title: ‘Be a sponge’: How one project executive brings education and construction together
construction.summary: Erin Kenney, a project executive for Suffolk in West Palm Beach, Florida, emphasized the power of mentorship and finding your niche.

construction.title: Transit, Amtrak cuts advance in House appropriations bill
construction.summary: The one-year bill cuts public transit funding by 22%, Amtrak by 69% and Capital Investment Grants by 78% for fiscal year 2027 but puts “a historic amount of funding” into bridges.

ai.title: What Parameter Golf taught us about AI-assisted research
ai.summary: Parameter Golf brought together 1,000+ participants and 2,000+ submissions to explore AI-assisted machine learning research, coding agents, quantization, and novel model design under strict constraints.
